programme.title: Supermarket Sweep

programme.title: ZZ Top - Live at Montreux
programme.desc: ZZ Top has made a number of visits to Montreux and this concert from 2013 is undoubtedly one of their finest live performances. The set list blends tracks from early seventies through their eighties blockbuster period and up to their most recent rele...

programme.title: John Mellencamp - Plain Spoken: Live in Chicago
programme.desc: John Mellencamp's career in music, spanning more than 42 years, has seen him transition from pop star to one of the most highly regarded songwriters of a generation.

programme.title: Florence + the Machine -- Live at Apple Music Festival
programme.desc: Following a week of class acts, Florence Welch celebrated both the final night of 2015's Apple Music Festival and her father's birthday with a flawless performance at London's Roundhouse.

programme.title: The Killers - Live at Rock Am Ring
programme.desc: The Killers perform at Rock Am Ring, a music festival that takes place annually at the Nürburgring Race Track in Western Germany, with tracks like When You Were Young and The World We Live In.

programme.title: Lana Del Rey - Live in London 2012
programme.desc: Born to Die is the second album by singer-songwriter Lana Del Rey, and the first to be officially released on January 27, 2012 and that would lead to global success; In countries such as the United Kingdom the album was the best selling of that year.

programme.title: Adele - iTunes Festival: Live in London
programme.desc: Qello Concerts by Stingray transforms any screen into the ultimate concert experience. Watch thousands of full-length concert films and music documentaries by yesterday's legends and today's icons on any device, anytime!

programme.title: Blondie -- Live in London 2014
programme.desc: Filmed at Roundhouse, London, giving all fans an experience; Blondie performs their hits including "Heart of Glass," "Euphoria," "Mile High" and many more.

programme.title: Coldplay - Austin City Limits
programme.desc: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas. The festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

programme.title: Wu-Tang Clan: Of Mics & Men
programme.desc: Twenty-five years in, a family looks to cement a legacy. 'Ol Dirty is dead. Everybody has gone solo. The 'W' is fading. Yet Wu-Tang Clan, their logo, their music and their message remain indelible fixtures in the history of music and America.

programme.title: Tina Turner: Videobiography
programme.desc: She's the Queen of Rock 'n' Roll! This definitive review of Tina Turner encompasses her entire career; from the difficult early years to superstardom, we leave no stone unturned.

programme.title: It's Showtime at the Apollo
programme.desc: It's Showtime at the Apollo is a syndicated music television show, first broadcast on September 12, 1987. The show features live performances from both professional and up-and-coming artists, and also features the Amateur Night competition.

programme.title: It's Showtime at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jazzy Jeff & The Fresh Prince perform, Entouch sing. Comedy by Chris Rock, and Lewis Dix

programme.title: Michael Jackson: Life, Death, and Legacy
programme.desc: In this film we take an intimate look into life of one of the worlds most successful entertainers of all time, a man dubbed as the King of Pop.

programme.title: Nick Cannon Presents: Future Superstars
programme.desc: The sixth episode tells the story of Long Beach rapper, DW Flame. After spending 18 months in jail, DW was exonerated and attacked the vocal booth with renewed hunger, detailing the adversities he had overcome.

programme.title: Nick Cannon Presents: Future Superstars
programme.desc: Jilly has all the makings of a star. We explore the challenges she faced thus far in the music industry and see how her determination is respected in the music space.

programme.title: Nick Cannon Presents: Future Superstars
programme.desc: TRAETWOTHREE is ready to show the world he is the next superstar out of South Central, blending dusky R&B, hip-hop swagger, and West Coast spirit.

programme.title: Nick Cannon Presents: Future Superstars
programme.desc: Nick Cannon introduces the world to his new R&B boy band, Pop Money. We'll witness the highs and lows of the band's first tour as well as their initial success as they release their debut EP.

programme.title: Nick Cannon Presents: Future Superstars
programme.desc: Nick Cannon sits down with VIBE editor-in-chief Datwon Thomas to make their predictions for the season 1 artists and where their careers will take them.

programme.title: The Story of James Brown
programme.desc: James Brown is one of the founders of funk music and is a major figure of 20th century music and dance. This program features interviews with the man himself, live performances, interviews with band members Bobby Byrd and Maceo Parker Jr.

programme.title: It's Showtime at the Apollo
programme.desc: It's Showtime at the Apollo is a syndicated music television show, first broadcast on September 12, 1987. The show features live performances from both professional and up-and-coming artists, and also features the Amateur Night competition.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: The day's sun is almost gone, but Bob Ross finds what's left of it in this unusual blend of late afternoon tones.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Palm trees sway in the warm breeze as Bob Ross teaches us to paint a beautiful, sunny exotic little ocean masterpiece.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Stand back in the shadows with Bob Ross as the last rays of sunshine sparkle on all they touch in this lovely waterside oval on black canvas.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Feel the soft coolness of late summer air and drink in the beauty of changing colors with Bob Ross as he creates this refreshing masterpiece.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Travel with Bob Ross to a secluded waterfall deep in the mountains, standing tall and proud. This magnificent scene instills comfort and joy.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a fantastic and truly unique geometric illusion of depth in today's lovely vision of pastel mountain magic.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Soft misty background, sweet snow-kissed cabin, and rich trees and bushes - all nestled down inside a winter oval painting by Bob Ross.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates an oval scene with warm brown and gold tones that captures the essence of a lonely forest river.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Join Bob Ross and experience the amazing beauty of the 'northern lights' phenomenon as they dance across the dark, dark sky - this time in an oval!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Nicholas Hankins: Bob Ross' Unfinished Season
programme.desc: Ancient rocks, lapping waves and a serene beach are the perfect setting for a flock of seagulls to hunt for their lunch.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Take a stroll into the woods with Bob Ross and experience the reflections of a jeweled sun bursting into the soft, vibrant day.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Go on a real nature hike with Bob Ross - follow the overgrown path deep in the woods and enjoy the silent beauty of the surrounding stillness.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Enjoy another black canvas painting -- Bob Ross creates the deep forest with tall majestic trees, rain puddles, and dark fresh air.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Take a stroll with Bob Ross, down to that perfect little fishing spot tucked away from the world, and relish the beauty of the day!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Watch as the sun comes up into the golden sky and creates an illusion of the calm morning sea in this painting by guest artist Audrey Golden.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Nicholas Hankins: Bob Ross' Unfinished Season
programme.desc: Nicholas Hankins paints a gnarled old tree adorned with Spanish moss as it stands sentinel over a moonlit stream.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross takes you to a beautiful lake in high country -- catch a glimpse of magnificent mountains, images echoed in the crystal clear water below.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: The fall breeze rustles in the trees as you rest your feet in crisp still waters. Discover the joys of nature in this Bob Ross painting.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Spend a half-hour with Bob Ross as he demonstrates the creation of the perfect brown-toned mountain scene - warm and wonderful!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Follow guest artist Annette Kowalski as she creates a beautiful hanging clay pot full of leaves and flowers using the Bob Ross painting method.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: A real viewer favorite, Bob Ross teaches us how to capture the exciting colors of autumn with trees, trees, and trees galore!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the beauty of Alaska on canvas: clear skies, soft cottony clouds, snow-covered mountains, and breathtaking crystal lake.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: It's almost fall, and Bob Ross captures the undaunted power and charm of a mighty brown mountain surveying its rustic surroundings.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Feel the cool breeze and admire the beauty of turbulent waves, jagged cliffs, and dark night skies -- Bob Ross paints a west coast seascape.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross illustrates the illusion of infinity as mountain and water fade into the distance, created in a lovely half-oval painting.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a beautiful golden warm waterfall scene, employing a lovely almost watercolor-looking effect. Come join in the fun!

programme.title: The Joy of Painting with Bob Ross
programme.desc: A real Bob Ross classic - the majestic snow-covered mountain, rolling foothills, leafy trees and bushes, and serene lake.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: DJ Steve Dahl sparks an anti-disco riot, the Cherry Sisters emerge as the worst vaudeville act of all time, and a rivalry between two actors turns violent. Featuring Colin Hanks, Jack McBrayer, David Koechner, Allison Tolman, Ben Folds and Patton Oswalt.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: The Ghost Army tricks German troops, a resistance movement grows within Japanese internment camps, and Adolf Hitler's nephew fights for the U.S. Featuring Randall Park, Lyric Lewis, Jack McBrayer and Aaron Takahashi.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: A victim of the Salem witch trials curses the town, one woman changes the way people celebrate Halloween, and Vlad the Impaler inspires the novel "Dracula." Featuring Seth Rogen, Allison Tolman, Jorma Taccone and Joel McHale.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Tiffany Haddish, Evan Rachel Wood, Busy Philipps and Mandy Moore pay tribute to the first woman to fight in the Revolutionary War and receive a pension, a museum curator who saved art from the Nazis and Civil War hero Clara Barton.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: A lawyer defends the lives of rats in court, Henry Bergh establishes the ASPCA, and horse learns to solve math problems. Featuring Mae Whitman, Claudia O'Doherty, Paul Scheer, Martin Starr and Samm Levine.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Taran Killam, Jerry O'Connell and Bob Odenkirk explore the lives of Jack Parsons, an occultist and rocket scientist; W.C. Minor, the man who helped write the Oxford English Dictionary; and Rasputin, adviser to Tsar Nicholas II.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Questlove, Method Man, Raven-Symone and more celebrate the early history of hip hop, the man known for inventing Motown, Berry Gordy, and "Star Trek" actress Nichelle Nichols's incredible activism on and off the TV screen.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: An Italian handyman steals the "Mona Lisa," the "Santa bandits" rob a bank in Texas, and Mossad agents capture a Nazi general in Argentina. Featuring Jack Black, Johnny Knoxville, Tony Hale, "Weird Al" Yankovic and Rachel Bloom.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Joan of Arc leads the French during the Hundred Years' War, Mongol leader Genghis Khan rises to power, and Mansa Musa leads a pilgrimage to Mecca. Featuring Vanessa Hudgens, Randall Park and Donald Faison.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Colin Hanks, David Harbour and Kimiko Glenn celebrate Fred Rogers and his fight for government-funded children's TV, the female journalist who helped take down John D. Rockefeller's oil monopoly and the designer of the Vietnam Veterans Memorial.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Tatiana Maslany, Kat Dennings and Ali Stroker salute Suffragettes who fought for their rights with jujitsu, the Birmingham Children's March and disability rights activists who organized America's longest sit-in of a federal building.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Drunk History investigates Agatha Christie's disappearance, the anonymous letter writer who terrorized Circleville and the mastermind behind America's only unsolved airplane hijacking, with Kirsten Dunst, Vanessa Bayer and Taran Killam.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Will Ferrell and Seth Rogen star in this episode about how teenage writer Mary Shelley created her legendary novel, "Frankenstein."

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: John Muir convinces Teddy Roosevelt to preserve Yosemite, journalist Marjory Stoneman Douglas fights to protect the Everglades, and Native American activists occupy Alcatraz.

programme.title: Drunk History
programme.desc: Moses Fleetwood Walker faces racism in the 19th-century MLB, the Chicago White Sox throw the World Series, and the Callaghan sisters inspire the film "A League of Their Own."

programme.title: Drunk History

programme.desc: See how NASA engineers met the challenge to build a spacecraft that can launch like a rocket and land like an airplane.

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: Explore basements from Pompeii to Pittsburgh; ancient Hittites, Phrygians, and Persians carved subterranean rooms for storage and shelter, Greeks and Romans valued them, Renaissance architects hid kitchens there, and Colonial Americans expanded use.

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: How padlocks and combination locks are made; biometric locks; cracking open a bank vault; how the government plans to keep people away from a nuclear waste site for the next 10,000 years.

programme.title: Modern Marvels

programme.title: Modern Marvels
programme.desc: The world's biggest and oldest flag manufacturer; inside the last US athletic shoe factory; the strangest form of alternate transportation; a hot toy company that believes it's imperative to keep their work here at home.

programme.title: Mysterious Earth
programme.desc: It is indisputable that despite their supposedly primitive technology, the societies that flourished 2,000 years before Christ were able to accomplish unbelievable feats that forever changed the understanding of the world for humankind.

programme.title: Mysterious Earth

programme.desc: President Richard Nixon's trip to China and the Watergate break-in.

programme.title: Origins
programme.desc: Three paths to destruction that are side effects of civilization.

programme.title: Origins
programme.desc: Three ways we exploit earth's resources to feed ourselves.

programme.desc: It is a quest that has fascinated scholars and adventurers for century after century. What happened to that treasure and centerpiece of ancient Jewish life — the Ark of the Covenant?

programme.title: Atomic: History Of The A-bomb
programme.desc: Documenting the discovery that changed everything.

programme.title: Dates That Made History
programme.desc: In our collective memory, 1492 is the year that divides the Middle Ages from Modern times. Why is this?

programme.title: Dates That Made History
programme.desc: The Tennis Court Oath was the key tipping point of the French Revolution, both from a symbolic and legal standpoint. On June 20, 1789, the deputies of the Third Estate gathered in the Hall of the Jeu de Paume at Versailles.

programme.title: The American West
programme.desc: Billy the Kid and Pat Garrett's cat-and-mouse game comes to a head. Wyatt Earp moves to Tombstone. Sitting Bull returns to America.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: Danger, Asteroids Ahead
programme.desc: The threat of an asteroid strike is a very real danger in our solar system, mankind is now in a position to defend the planet from such an eventuality.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: Atmosphere
programme.desc: Our atmosphere is an unbelievably complex machine that protects us from the vacuum of space, shelters us from harmful radiation and allows us and the biosphere to breath; we need to learn much more about it so we can better it.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: Atmosphere
programme.desc: Our atmosphere is an unbelievably complex machine that protects us from the vacuum of space, shelters us from harmful radiation and allows us and the biosphere to breath; we need to learn much more about it so we can better it.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: Living Planet
programme.desc: We have been observing Earth for 40 years now, in the last 20 we have focus with intensity on the planet with new technologies and capabilities, we have accumulated a mass of data that is now revealing a complex and ever changing living planet.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: The Moons of Olympus
programme.desc: There are many more worlds in our solar system than just the planets. The mysterious moons of the gas giants may hold much more than we ever imagined. Each one is unique, and each one deserves a scrutinous eye.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: The Gas Giants
programme.desc: In the cold, dark expanse of our solar system, beyond the asteroid belt, lie the giant planets. Some can be seen with the naked eye, others have been visited by our camera. What secrets do they hold?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: Danger, Asteroids Ahead
programme.desc: The threat of an asteroid strike is a very real danger in our solar system, mankind is now in a position to defend the planet from such an eventuality.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier: First Light
programme.desc: The Hubble Telescope is nearing its end, the replacements are ready to go, ready to peer further back in time and space than can be imagined.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Night Fire
programme.desc: The Northern Lights are more than just beautiful; they tell us about the make up of our planet, our sun, and may even be a signpost for the possibility of life on other planets.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Virtual Universe
programme.desc: Our understanding of the cosmos is limited by the amount of time we have to see it. Fortunately, computer simulations can help us see how galaxies interact over millions of years, and even how dark matter structures the universe.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Stargazer's Paradise
programme.desc: The Atacama desert is a perfect place for watching the night sky. With still winds and only a handful of cloudy nights each year, it allows scientists to get the absolute most out of their tools.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Hubble's Renaissance
programme.desc: Before we can reach space, we must first be able to see it. Hundreds of years ago, the simple telescope was used to give us a seemingly closer vantage. Today, we use the awe-inspiring, and super powerful, Hubble Space Telescope.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Superscopes
programme.desc: Discover the most powerful telescopes on the planet as they search for the mysteries of the universe.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Cosmic Rainbow
programme.desc: The human eye can only see visible light, a small spectrum of electromagnetic waves. Today, we have instruments that let us perceive all wavelengths.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Seasons of Saturn
programme.desc: Saturn is the second largest planet and has an astonishing 60 moons in its orbit. Thanks to the Cassini mission, it’s also one of the most studied and most photographed planets in our neighborhood.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Vesta Unveiled
programme.desc: With the Dawn missions exploration of the asteroid Vesta and the dwarf planet Ceres, asteroids are a hot topic. See what these incredible missions have uncovered.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Water Worlds
programme.desc: They key ingredient to life in the universe is water. Until recently we thought Earth was the only place in the solar system to have any water at all. Now that probes are peering around nearby worlds we are finding that is just not the case.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Gravity
programme.desc: Gravity has long been a force that has intrigued scientists. Now, for the first time scientists are probing to detect gravity waves in space as well as mapping the gravity patterns of the moon.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Science and the Shuttle
programme.desc: For more than 25 years the shuttle was our ticket to space, and our laboratory in orbit. Uncover the legacy of this amazing machine, and see what lies ahead.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Landing on Mars
programme.desc: Mars sparks our curiosity like no other planet because it is so reachable. Despite having landed there, most of the information we gather is still done by instruments in the planet's orbit.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Milky Way
programme.desc: This episode traces our view of the Milky Way, from the mythology of ancient skywatchers to the efforts of scientists to map it from the inside out.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: The Big Bang
programme.desc: When it comes to the birth of the universe, not even the most creative work of imagination can rival what science has revealed about the Big Bang.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: The Hot Zone
programme.desc: Mercury is one of Earth's closest neighbors, but until very recent exploratory missions, it eluded astronomers' best efforts to learn its secrets.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Cosmic Rays
programme.desc: Cosmic rays can tell us much about the universe. They are not simply another form of light, but carriers of information about powerful and unseen processes at work thousands or even millions of light years away.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: The Fermi Paradox
programme.desc: The Fermi Paradox asks the ultimate question: why have alien civilizations not presented themselves?

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Landsat
programme.desc: An advanced $900 million planetary explorer designed to peer across multiple wavelengths to reveal intricate details undetectable by the human eye.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Eye of the Beholder
programme.desc: The night sky has always been a source of wonder and mystery. In the quest to explore the universe, our greatest ally is the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: Secrets of War

programme.title: Secrets of War
programme.desc: The secrets behind Ho Chi Minh's control of Vietnam and his ability to force outside forces out of his country during his three-decade reign.

programme.title: Greatest Tank Battles

programme.title: Battleplan

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team visit Durham Cathedral where treasures include a sweetheart brooch linked to the Grenadier Guards, a giant Victorian penknife, and secret documents that offer new information about the D-Day landings.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team return to Waltham Forest Town Hall where a busy day reveals an art deco pendant with a hint of Ancient Egypt, a haul of charming tiles discovered behind a chimney and a purse that saved the life of a German soldier in the First World War.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team visit the Liverpool Metropolitan Cathedral, where treasures include a miniature kitchen range, a piece of the city's heritage found in a French flea market and a sextant used at sea in the race to rescue survivors of the Titanic.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team makes a return visit to Liverpool Metropolitan Cathedral, where fascinating finds include a miniature piano with a secret, a sketch by LS Lowry and a mysterious chest found bricked up in the crypt.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A collection of Chairman Mao memorabilia from China, a funeral standard believed to have been taken from Oliver Cromwell's coffin and a purse that hides a secret weapon are found in Belton House.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: On a second visit to Belton House in Lincolnshire, a ceramic model of London Zoo's first elephant, a 400-year-old Japanese chest and a walnut with a secret are found.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Make-up from the glamorous era of the flappers, a mysterious letterbox and a stuffed dog with an aristocratic pedigree are some treasures found in Lowther Castle in Cumbria.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, the TAPS team is on Rose Island to investigate their haunted lighthouse. This island housed cholera victims exiled from Newport, and many of the sick never returned to the mainland.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS is asked to investigate the abandoned Milton School in Alton, Ill. where some studentsare said to still be walking through the halls.
